Package glide.api.commands

Interface ConnectionManagementCommands

  • All Known Implementing Classes:
    GlideClient
    public interface ConnectionManagementCommands
    Supports commands for the "Connection Management" group for a standalone client.
    See Also:
    Connection Management Commands

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> clientGetName()
      Gets the name of the current connection.
      java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.Long> clientId()
      Gets the current connection id.
      java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<GlideString> echo​(GlideString message)
      Echoes the provided message back.
      java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> echo​(java.lang.String message)
      Echoes the provided message back.
      java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> ping()
      Pings the server.
      java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<GlideString> ping​(GlideString message)
      Pings the server.
      java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> ping​(java.lang.String message)
      Pings the server.
      java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> select​(long index)
      Changes the currently selected database.

    • Method Detail

      • ping

        java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> ping()
        Pings the server.
        Returns:
        String with "PONG".
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 String payload = client.ping().get();
 assert payload.equals("PONG");

      • ping

        java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> ping​(java.lang.String message)
        Pings the server.
        Parameters:
        message - The server will respond with a copy of the message.
        Returns:
        String with a copy of the argument message.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 String payload = client.ping("GLIDE").get();
 assert payload.equals("GLIDE");

      • ping

        java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<GlideString> ping​(GlideString message)
        Pings the server.
        Parameters:
        message - The server will respond with a copy of the message.
        Returns:
        GlideString with a copy of the argument message.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 GlideString payload = client.ping(gs("GLIDE")).get();
 assert payload.equals(gs("GLIDE"));

      • clientId

        java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.Long> clientId()
        Gets the current connection id.
        Returns:
        The id of the client.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 Long id = client.clientId().get();
 assert id > 0;

      • clientGetName

        java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> clientGetName()
        Gets the name of the current connection.
        Returns:
        The name of the client connection as a string if a name is set, or null if no name is assigned.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 String clientName = client.clientGetName().get();
 assert clientName != null;

      • echo

        java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> echo​(java.lang.String message)
        Echoes the provided message back.
        Parameters:
        message - The message to be echoed back.
        Returns:
        The provided message.
        See Also:
        Example: 
        
 String payload = client.echo("GLIDE").get();
 assert payload.equals("GLIDE");

      • echo

        java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<GlideString> echo​(GlideString message)
        Echoes the provided message back.
        Parameters:
        message - The message to be echoed back.
        Returns:
        The provided message.
        See Also:
        Example: 
        
 GlideString payload = client.echo(gs("GLIDE")).get();
 assert payload.equals(gs("GLIDE"));

      • select

        java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture<java.lang.String> select​(long index)
        Changes the currently selected database.

        WARNING: This command is NOT RECOMMENDED for production use. Upon reconnection, the client will revert to the database_id specified in the client configuration (default: 0), NOT the database selected via this command.

        RECOMMENDED APPROACH: Use the database_id parameter in client configuration instead:

        RECOMMENDED EXAMPLE: 

        
 GlideClient client = GlideClient.createClient(
     GlideClientConfiguration.builder()
         .address(NodeAddress.builder().host("localhost").port(6379).build())
         .databaseId(5)  // Recommended: persists across reconnections
         .build()
 ).get();
        Parameters:
        index - The index of the database to select.
        Returns:
        A simple OK response.
        See Also:
        valkey.io for details.
        Example:
        
 String response = regularClient.select(0).get();
 assert response.equals("OK");
